metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC #1 that asserts each workflow's `name:` field matches the locked table — e.g. a `grep`-based one-liner or a new test assertion so the check is part of CI.
evidence: 'make lint passes (actionlint over all workflows)' — actionlint does not check name: casing or content against a table
Locked table values are listed in the What section prose but no test or script is referenced to assert them post-change
findings.finding: AC #1 ('Every workflow has a descriptive Title Case `name:` per the locked table') has no proving command that validates `name:` field content against the locked table. `make lint` (actionlint) validates YAML syntax and action-pinning, not that `name:` values are Title Case and match the enumerated table. None of the listed tests (test_ci_workflow_parity.py, test_external_canary.py, test_verify_opcert_heldout.py, test_ci_actionlint_scope.py) are described as asserting `name:` values. The criterion is reachable by the described steps, but done-ness is unverifiable in-session without a concrete check.
scenarios: An executor changes name: values but makes a typo or uses a different capitalisation convention; `make lint` passes and the error is undetected.
A reviewer cannot run a single command to confirm all 11 name: values match the locked table.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to the plan (and optionally to a test): `python - <<'EOF'
import yaml, pathlib, sys
expected = {
  'gerrit-verify.yaml': 'Gerrit Verified Gate',
  'release.yml': 'Release',
  # ... rest of table
}
for f, name in expected.items():
    doc = yaml.safe_load(pathlib.Path(f'.github/workflows/{f}').read_text())
    assert doc['name'] == name, f'{f}: got {doc["name"]!r}'
print('All name: fields match locked table')
EOF`

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC #3, e.g. `test ! -f .github/workflows/eval-plan-review-container.yml && grep -rL 'eval-plan-review-container' src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md` (exit-nonzero on failure), or add an assertion to an existing test.
evidence: 'make lint passes (actionlint over all workflows)' — actionlint operates on present files; it does not detect a file that should have been deleted
'test_ci_actionlint_scope.py' — this test scopes actionlint coverage but does not assert that eval-plan-review-container.yml is absent
No grep/ls command prescribed to confirm deletion and README cleanup
findings.finding: AC #3 ('eval-plan-review-container.yml deleted; its README reference removed') has no explicit proving command among the listed tests or lint steps. The five named tests do not obviously assert the file's absence or that the README no longer references it, so a reviewer cannot mechanically confirm AC #3 is satisfied.
scenarios: An executor forgets to delete the file or remove its README line; all named tests still pass and the gap goes unnoticed until the Actions UI still shows the stale workflow.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the AC bullet: 'Verify with: `test ! -f .github/workflows/eval-plan-review-container.yml` and `! grep -q eval-plan-review-container src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a repo-wide grep command to the plan (e.g. `grep -r 'eval\.yml\|eval-plan-review-container\|verify-identity\.yaml' --include='*.md' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.py' .`) so the executor can confirm no stray references remain after the five listed updates.
evidence: 'src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md ... tests/unit/test_verify_opcert_heldout.py + docs/identity.md + docs/adr/0049-opcert-asymmetric.md' — only five paths enumerated
AC #2 says 'all in-repo references updated' implying exhaustiveness, but the step only names five known files
findings.finding: The plan lists specific known files for reference updates but does not prescribe a repo-wide search to catch any additional occurrences of 'eval.yml', 'eval-plan-review-container.yml', or 'verify-identity.yaml'. An executor following only the enumerated list could miss references in other docs, scripts, or CI configs.
scenarios: A CI config, a script, or another doc also references 'eval.yml' by name; the executor updates only the five listed files and misses the stray reference, causing a broken link or CI failure.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Update references' bullet: 'After updating the listed files, run `grep -rn "eval\.yml\|eval-plan-review-container\|verify-identity\.yaml" --include="*.md" --include="*.yml" --include="*.yaml" --include="*.py" .` and resolve any additional hits.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'CI workflow naming conventions' section to AGENTS.md (or CONTRIBUTING.md) capturing: filenames lowercase-with-hyphens + .yml only; `name:` short Title Case; reusable workflows use leading-underscore prefix; gerrit-verify.yaml and release.yml are locked exceptions with their reasons — so the standard survives beyond this plan ticket.
evidence: Plan defines a 'locked table' and naming standard inline but does not mention updating AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or creating an ADR to persist the convention.
The whole motivation of the change is establishing a consistent standard — but that standard lives only in a closed plan ticket after merge.
'Adopt the researched standard used by popular repos + GitHub docs' — this research and decision is not captured anywhere permanent.
findings.finding: The new workflow-naming convention (lowercase-with-hyphens filenames, .yml extension, Title Case `name:` fields, underscore-prefix for reusables, locked-exception rationale) is defined only in the ephemeral plan document, not in any persistent living doc (AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or a new ADR). Future contributors adding workflows will have no authoritative reference and may drift back to the old inconsistent patterns.
findings.location: ## What section (naming convention definition) and ## Locked filenames
scenarios: A contributor adds a new workflow six months later, doesn't know the convention, uses a non-Title-Case name or .yaml extension, and the inconsistency silently re-accumulates.
A reviewer approving a new workflow has no normative doc to point to when asking for a name fix.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section to `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` or `AGENTS.md` under a heading like `## CI Workflow Naming Conventions` that reproduces the locked table and rationale in 10–15 lines. Reference it from the PR description so reviewers know where to look for future submissions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-container.eval.yaml` to the reference-update list and update its description of 'the eval CI' to reflect the deletion of eval-plan-review-container.yml (e.g. remove or reword the 'the gated, path-scoped, NON-BLOCKING semantic eval CI' sentence at lines 18-19).
evidence: src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-container.eval.yaml:18-19 reads: 'Validate offline: `rebar prompt eval plan-review-container`. The live model run needs the [eval] extra + credentials (the gated, path-scoped, NON-BLOCKING semantic eval CI; a human-reviewed development gate for S4/S5 — see eval_specs/README.plan-review.md).'

The plan lists only README.plan-review.md as needing the 'drop container ref' update, but plan-review-container.eval.yaml contains a cross-reference to eval-plan-review-container.yml's role as 'the eval CI' that will be stale after deletion.

Search for 'eval-plan-review-container' finds README.plan-review.md:44 explicitly named in the plan, but plan-review-container.eval.yaml:18-19 contains an implicit reference to that same workflow as 'the eval CI' that is not identified in the plan's reference list.
findings.finding: plan-review-container.eval.yaml references eval-plan-review-container.yml (the workflow being deleted) as 'the gated, path-scoped, NON-BLOCKING semantic eval CI', but the plan's reference-update list does not include this file.
findings.location: What section, reference update list: 'src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md (eval->prompt-eval, drop container ref)'
scenarios: After eval-plan-review-container.yml is deleted, plan-review-container.eval.yaml still instructs users to 'see eval_specs/README.plan-review.md' for a workflow that no longer exists, leaving stale documentation in the standing eval spec.
A developer following plan-review-container.eval.yaml's instructions to use 'the gated, path-scoped, NON-BLOCKING semantic eval CI' will find no such workflow.
findings.suggested_fix: In plan-review-container.eval.yaml lines 18-19, replace the reference to the deleted workflow with a note that the live semantic eval is now run via `rebar prompt eval plan-review-container` with the [eval] extra directly, or remove the sentence pointing to the deleted workflow file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `rebar.toml` (line 79, comment reference to `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml`) to the reference-update list for the `verify-identity.yaml → verify-identity.yml` rename.
evidence: rebar.toml line 79: `# `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml` runs `rebar verify-identity` with NO forced`
Plan's update list for .yaml→.yml rename names only: README.plan-review.md, test_verify_opcert_heldout.py, docs/identity.md, docs/adr/0049-opcert-asymmetric.md — rebar.toml absent
findings.finding: rebar.toml line 79 contains a comment referencing `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml` by its old .yaml path, but rebar.toml is not listed in the plan's reference-update set for the verify-identity.yaml → verify-identity.yml rename.
findings.location: What section — reference update list: 'tests/unit/test_verify_opcert_heldout.py + docs/identity.md + docs/adr/0049-opcert-asymmetric.md (.yaml->.yml on verify-identity)'
scenarios: After the rename lands, rebar.toml still says verify-identity.yaml; a reader or automation scanning for workflow filename references will find a stale comment, and a grep for 'verify-identity.yaml' in the repo will still hit.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, extend the update list to: `rebar.toml` (comment on line 79: `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml` → `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yml`)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the 'Update references' bullet in What to explicitly list test_ci_workflow_parity.py, test_external_canary.py, and test_ci_actionlint_scope.py (or add a note that all workflow-pinning tests must be audited for filename references after each rename/deletion).
evidence: 'Update references: src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md (eval->prompt-eval, drop container ref); tests/unit/test_verify_opcert_heldout.py + docs/identity.md + docs/adr/0049-opcert-asymmetric.md (.yaml->.yml on verify-identity)'
AC #5: 'make lint passes … and the workflow-pinning tests pass: test_ci_workflow_parity.py, test_external_canary.py, test_verify_opcert_heldout.py, test_ci_actionlint_scope.py'
ABSENCE: test_ci_workflow_parity.py, test_external_canary.py, test_ci_actionlint_scope.py not mentioned in the 'Update references' bullet despite being named in AC #5
findings.finding: The 'What' section's reference-update list explicitly names only four locations (README.plan-review.md, test_verify_opcert_heldout.py, docs/identity.md, docs/adr/0049-opcert-asymmetric.md), but AC #5 requires three additional test files (test_ci_workflow_parity.py, test_external_canary.py, test_ci_actionlint_scope.py) to pass — these tests are likely to reference renamed or deleted workflow filenames (eval.yml → prompt-eval.yml; eval-plan-review-container.yml deleted) and are absent from the update list, leaving their needed changes uncovered in the plan body.
scenarios: An implementer following the What section's reference list to the letter skips updating test_ci_workflow_parity.py; that test fails on the renamed/deleted file, blocking the Verified vote.
test_ci_actionlint_scope.py enumerates expected workflow files; eval-plan-review-container.yml deletion or eval.yml rename would cause it to fail if not updated.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the 'Update references' bullet: '+ tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py, tests/unit/test_external_canary.py, tests/unit/test_ci_actionlint_scope.py (audit for eval.yml / eval-plan-review-container.yml references; update to prompt-eval.yml / remove as appropriate).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., 'revert the single commit via `git revert`; re-push through Gerrit') for the file deletion and renames so reversibility is documented, not implicit.
evidence: 'Delete eval-plan-review-container.yml (da34 closed; opt-in non-blocking, not a required check)' — deletion is justified but no back-out plan is stated
No 'rollback', 'revert', or 'back-out' language appears anywhere in the plan
AC list has no 'rollback if Gerrit rejects mid-sequence' or 'restore from git history' step
findings.finding: The plan has no rollback/back-out path for the file deletion (eval-plan-review-container.yml) or the renames (eval.yml→prompt-eval.yml, verify-identity.yaml→verify-identity.yml). T4 requires an explicit reversibility mechanism, not just acknowledgement of the destructive step.
scenarios: A downstream consumer (e.g., a scheduled trigger or external tool) turns out to depend on eval-plan-review-container.yml after deletion; no recovery path is described
Renaming eval.yml mid-deploy breaks a required status check before the rename lands atomically; plan gives no recovery step
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'All changes land in a single Gerrit commit; back-out is `git revert <sha>` pushed through Gerrit, which restores all deleted/renamed files atomically. No database or external-state migration is involved.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Audit GitHub branch protection rules and repository rulesets for references to `eval.yml`, `verify-identity.yaml`, and any job names within those workflows; document that no required-status-check entries point to the old identifiers (or update them as part of this change).
evidence: 'Rename files: eval.yml -> prompt-eval.yml; verify-identity.yaml -> verify-identity.yml'
Plan updates only in-repo file references; GitHub repository settings (branch protection rules, required status checks) are not files and are not mentioned

Contrast with the explicit acknowledgement for gerrit-verify.yaml ('Gerrit g2p dispatches by filename substring') and release.yml ('PyPI OIDC trusted-publisher subject matches the workflow filename') — the same class of external-config risk is not checked for the renamed files
findings.finding: The plan does not acknowledge that GitHub branch protection required-status-checks or other GitHub-managed repo settings (not in-repo files) may reference the old workflow filenames (eval.yml, verify-identity.yaml) or job names. Renaming these files silently removes the old status-check identifier from GitHub's perspective, potentially unblocking or breaking PRs that depended on those checks.
scenarios: A branch protection rule requires the status check named after the old workflow file or a job within eval.yml; after rename the check silently disappears from the required list, allowing unreviewed merges
verify-identity.yaml rename changes the workflow's run identifier; any org-level ruleset referencing it by filename breaks silently

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `README.plan-review.md:40` (the `eval.yml` → `prompt-eval.yml` reference in the offline-discipline paragraph) to the explicit reference-update inventory in the 'What' section, separate from the 'drop container ref' instruction.
evidence: README.plan-review.md line 40: 'offline check unchanged: `eval.yml`'s `eval-discipline` job validates *every* packaged'

Plan 'What' section lists only these four reference update targets: README.plan-review.md (eval->prompt-eval, drop container ref), tests/unit/test_verify_opcert_heldout.py, docs/identity.md, docs/adr/0049-opcert-asymmetric.md

The plan describes the README update as 'eval->prompt-eval, drop container ref' but this is ambiguous — it may or may not cover the inline `eval.yml` string at line 40 (as opposed to the explicit `eval-plan-review-container.yml` mention at line 44)
Tool-verified: search_files confirmed 'eval.yml' appears at README.plan-review.md:40 and the plan does not explicitly list it in the reference-update inventory
findings.finding: README.plan-review.md line 40 contains a hardcoded 'eval.yml' workflow filename reference that becomes a stale/broken documentation pointer after the eval.yml → prompt-eval.yml rename. The plan states it will update README.plan-review.md to 'drop container ref', but only the eval-plan-review-container.yml reference (line 44) is described as the target update — the eval.yml reference at line 40 is not listed in the plan's reference-update inventory, leaving a pink-elephant instruction-locality gap in the LLM eval documentation.
scenarios: An implementer reads the 'drop container ref' instruction, removes the line 44 mention of eval-plan-review-container.yml, and ships — leaving the line 40 'eval.yml' reference stale, causing the README to document a non-existent workflow filename.

Future contributors reading README.plan-review.md see 'eval.yml's eval-discipline job' and look for a workflow named eval.yml, which no longer exists, degrading the documentation's instructional accuracy for the LLM eval system.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section reference-update list, change `README.plan-review.md (eval->prompt-eval, drop container ref)` to `README.plan-review.md (eval->prompt-eval in the offline-discipline paragraph at line 40; drop eval-plan-review-container.yml reference at line 44)` so both stale strings are explicitly targeted.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
